subject: English
title: A Question of Trust – Medium SNS

key_points: The story is written by Victor Canning and focuses on trust and moral conflict
Horace Danby lives a double life as a respectable locksmith and secret thief
He justifies stealing by limiting it to once a year and targeting the rich
Horace carefully plans his robbery at Shotover Grange
The young lady uses confidence and deception to manipulate Horace
Horace’s fear of prison makes him obey the young lady
Removing gloves becomes the turning point of the story
Fingerprints act as crucial evidence against Horace
The story disproves the idea of honour among thieves
Irony is strongly used to convey the moral lesson
characters: Horace Danby – Intelligent, careful, but morally conflicted
The Young Lady – Psychologically strong, manipulative, and ruthless
Sherry – Symbol of false security and normalcy
important_events: Horace studies the house and servants’ routine
He sneezes due to hay fever, showing human weakness
The young lady pretends to forget the safe’s combination
Horace opens the safe without gloves
Police arrest Horace using fingerprint evidence
exam_tips: Use irony as a key point in medium answers
Mention fingerprints as the decisive factor
Link the young lady’s confidence with deception
Support answers with suitable examples from the story
